items: Improved durable memory workflow after a recall failure: adopted immediate logging for user choices, preferences, standing asks, fixes, and process changes.
Added/confirmed use of active-context tracking so future sessions can recover current priorities quickly.
Confirmed and documented a persistent outstanding-items/task-list requirement instead of relying on chat history.
Recovered Jon’s previously chosen surprise project and restored it to durable memory.
Updated the project’s canonical name from **Link Valet** to **Memory Palace**.
Captured the canonical scope for **Memory Palace**: low-friction link saving with title/summary capture, tagging/classification, browsing, and fuzzy resurfacing.
Logged a new long-running project request: a public web page that extracts article body text from a news URL, with a path to improve extraction reliability over time.
Documented a failure lesson: check memory first, admit gaps plainly, and fix the memory system instead of bluffing.
